issues.description: Template literal used to combine className prop with static Tailwind classes instead of cn() utility.
issues.rule: Use cn() for combining dynamic/conditional classes
issues.fix: Replace template literal with cn(): className={cn('invisible absolute whitespace-pre', className)}

issues.description: Template literal used to construct a dynamic Tailwind class string inside cn() call. The color value comes from PRODUCT_CATEGORIES constants (CSS variables like 'var(--pink)'), which are used as inline class strings. However, the pattern `${color} text-foreground` is building a class string from a CSS variable value, not a Tailwind class — this may not work as intended since CSS variable references are not Tailwind utility class names.
issues.rule: Template literal class construction should use cn() and Tailwind-valid classes
issues.fix: Verify that the 'color' value in PRODUCT_CATEGORIES resolves to a valid Tailwind class (not a CSS var()). If colors are CSS vars, use style={{ backgroundColor: color }} for the active background instead of embedding CSS var strings in className.

patterns.description: @source directives are correctly configured in all 7 apps (store, admin, auth, landing, payments, playground, studio). All include both self-scan and ui package scan directives.
All apps import from 'ui/globals' for shared theme, ensuring CSS variable consistency across apps. globals.css files are identical in all apps.
No raw Tailwind palette colors (red-*, green-*, blue-*, gray-*, slate-*, etc.) found in any .ts/.tsx files. All color usage goes through semantic tokens.

Category color theming uses CSS variables (var(--pink), var(--mint), etc.) via a centralized categoryConstants.ts in packages/shared. Inline styles using theme.bg, theme.bgLight, theme.foreground are intentional — they apply dynamic CSS variable values computed at runtime from category data, which cannot be expressed as static Tailwind classes.
cn() utility is consistently used for conditional class combinations throughout the codebase.
